Isabella had to make the hardest decision of her life. Her husband had become violent, and the last incident left her terrified. She knew, deep in her bones, that if she stayed, she might not survive. So she took her two children—Gabriel who was seven, and Olivia who was four—and she left everything behind. They had nowhere to go. No savings. No job. No family nearby. But she had to leave. It was not safe anymore. This is the story of how Isabella rebuilt her life from nothing, how she overcame homelessness and fear, and how she discovered that she was worthy of being seen.
